summary:
The Maternal and Child Health Stillbirth Prevention Act of 2024 amends title V of the Social Security Act to clarify that federal maternal and child health funds may be used for stillbirth prevention and related research.

The law changes section 501(a) of the Social Security Act, which governs purposes for maternal and child health services funding. It adds reducing the incidence of stillbirth to the listed purposes of funds used to reduce infant mortality and improve child health. It also specifies that funds may be used for evidence-based programs and activities, and for outcome research, aimed at reducing stillbirth.

The Act lists examples of permissible stillbirth prevention efforts, including tracking and awareness of fetal movements; improving birth timing for pregnancies with risk factors; initiatives encouraging safe sleeping positions during pregnancy; screening and surveillance for fetal growth restriction; smoking cessation efforts during pregnancy; community-based programs providing home visits or other support; and other research or evidence-based programming to prevent stillbirths.

The Act does not create a new grant program or specify new funding amounts. It contains no separate effective date or implementation deadline.
